commands.repair_reason: The protected maintainer label, missing external real-behavior proof, and security-doc mismatch require human/contributor follow-up rather than an automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The code narrows proxy-scoped access, but the PR leaves security-sensitive trusted-proxy docs describing the old pairing boundary.; Review finding: [P2] Update the trusted-proxy Control UI docs — src/gateway/server/ws-connection/co... (sha=fad1f24c11ceabb9ff59fbaf13350bdb81e89916)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes trusted-proxy Control UI WebSocket auth so fresh device identities must complete pairing before requested operator scopes are honored, clears unbound scopes for device-less proxy sessions, and updates focused gateway auth tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has a clear source-level path: `trustedProxyAuthOk` skips Control UI pairing, and the existing suite asserts an unpaired trusted-proxy device identity keeps operator scopes and can run an admin RPC; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label, missing external real-behavior proof, and security-doc mismatch require human/contributor follow-up rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Require Control UI pairing before proxy-scoped access [AI]
